kernel_mount.result: macOS APFS kernel extension auto-mounted the CAB containers read-write and committed to them
kernel_mount.evidence: the 8 TiB container's fsck_apfs first refused -n with 'container /dev/rdisk4 is mounted with write access'; the 8 TiB and 24 TB volume superblocks record 'last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1)' and their checkpoints advanced to transaction ID 4 (writer emits xid 2, the kernel commit advanced to 4)

cab_inplace_commit.tier: A2-2 CAB-tier in-place copy-on-write commit (unified rotation group stride 4: cib0 + chunk0-bitmap + cab0 + boundary-chunk-bitmap)
cab_inplace_commit.container_image: /xfs/cabA.apfs FORMAT (8 TiB, cab_count 2) then three chained commit-image-file-insert commits cabA->cabB->cabC->cabD (xid 2->3->4->5)
cab_inplace_commit.design: the spaceman addresses cibs through a cab-address array, so cab 0 rotates with cib 0 every commit (cab_cib_addr[0] re-pointed at the rotated cib 0, the spaceman cab-array re-pointed at the rotated cab 0); the boundary chunk's bitmap rides at group offset 2 + cabSlot. Same unified-group machinery as the metadata-overflow tier, stride 4.
cab_inplace_commit.linux_apfsck: apfsck -w clean on the CAB FORMAT and clean (only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' apfsck-vs-Apple divergence) after each of the three chained CAB in-place commits

apple_fsck_apfs.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk5 (cabD, unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 5 (three chained commits); the macOS kernel auto-mounted the CAB container read-write and CONTINUED the checkpoint to transaction ID 7 (two kernel commits past the writer) - the authority apfsck cannot model
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_read_files: the kernel read all three files inserted by the chained CAB in-place commits: a2cab1.txt (24 B), a2cab2.txt (26 B), a2cab3.txt (26 B 'cab-a2-cert-payload-three'), sizes and contents matching the payloads
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk5 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk5s1 (UUID 4BDBF6B5-AA21-466F-A09D-243669C517A2) appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_provenance: The volume CABA was formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1).
apple_fsck_apfs.evidence: apfs-cab-inplace-commit-fsck-clean.png (fsck verdict), apfs-cab-inplace-commit-kernel-read-files.png (kernel ls of the 3 inserted files)

linux_apfsck_cross_check.fail_closed: 100 TiB correctly blocked by the writer (ip-bitmap ring + cab-address array overflow the spaceman block; certified CAB ceiling ~48 TiB)
